carry_forward.detail: Device code objects and exported symbols identical (Windows-only -fPIC fix does not affect Linux builds)
Device code objects and exported symbols identical (Windows-only -fPIC fix does not affect Linux builds)

windows-gfx1101.blocked_reason: Value-head numerical defect identical to gfx1151: builds+runs (344 targets, benchmark clean, 8/8 CPU gtest), policy bit-identical (~4.6e-07) but value wrong 4.4e-02 absolute (sign flips) at atol=1e-3 bar. Defect appears identically on gfx1201 under same TheRock ROCm 7.14 Windows SDK. Exhaustive investigation: graph capture off, use_gemm_ex=false, Conv1Layer loop replacement, standalone BLAS tests all PASS -- BLAS is correct for all relevant dimensions. Root cause unresolved; suspected TheRock ROCm 7.14 Windows HIP runtime issue in custom kernel path. Linux gfx90a+gfx1100 pass the same check on ROCm 7.2.1.

windows-gfx1201.blocked_reason: Value-head numerical defect identical to gfx1151 and gfx1101: builds+runs (344 targets, benchmark clean, 8/8 CPU gtest), policy bit-identical (~4.6e-07) but value wrong 4.4e-02 absolute (sign flips) at atol=1e-3 bar. Defect appears identically on gfx1101 under same TheRock ROCm 7.14 Windows SDK. Exhaustive investigation: graph capture off, use_gemm_ex=false, Conv1Layer loop replacement, standalone BLAS tests all PASS -- BLAS is correct for all relevant dimensions. Root cause unresolved; suspected TheRock ROCm 7.14 Windows HIP runtime issue in custom kernel path. Linux gfx90a+gfx1100 pass the same check on ROCm 7.2.1.

windows-gfx1151.blocked_reason: Value-head numerical defect on gfx1151 only: builds+runs (hip backend benchmark clean), policy head bit-identical to blas (~1e-6) but value head off 4-6e-2 absolute with sign flips (rel up to 2.0) at the atol=1e-3 bar that gfx90a+gfx1100 pass. Localized to the value head (trunk/policy provably correct); leading hypothesis is a gfx1151 rocBLAS/Tensile fallback kernel for the value GEMM shape or the value SE/globalAvgPool reduction. Prior session did not converge. See notes.md windows-gfx1151 section.
